description: Named NPCs for the game, extracted from Nannymud lore and extended for gameplay purposes. Merchants, quest-givers, guild trainers, and flavor characters.

categories.merchant: Buys and sells items. Prices modified by reputation.
categories.banker: Stores gold and items across sessions. Charges 1% withdrawal fee.
categories.trainer: Teaches guild abilities on level-up. Must visit to unlock new skills.
categories.quest-giver: Offers one or more quests from quests.json.
categories.guard: Attacks PK-flagged players on sight in safe hubs.
categories.royal: Flavor NPC, rarely quest-givers for top-tier content.
categories.flavor: Ambient NPC, no mechanical function. Populates hubs.

npcs.lore: The local god. Creator of Nanny. Appears only to those who have mastered all six base guilds and completed the High Quest. 'Beware his wrath.'
npcs.gameplayRole: Endgame quest-giver. Triggers the Master class unlock chain.
npcs.voiceDirection: Deep, amused, faintly accented. Never angry — merely disappointed, which is worse.

npcs.lore: Ancient wizard, member of the original four High Wizards. Teaches the Mages Guild abilities.
npcs.gameplayRole: Mage class trainer. Sells mage-specific consumables.

npcs.lore: Keeper of the Central Bank of Ghan-Toth. Stern, impartial, unbribable.
npcs.gameplayRole: Primary banker. Stores items (80 slot vault) and gold. Withdrawal fee 1%.

npcs.lore: Master armorer and weaponsmith. His forge never cools. Hands are scarred but his eye is true.
npcs.gameplayRole: Primary weapon/armor merchant. Sells tier 1-3 gear. Buys loot at 40% value.

npcs.lore: Divination specialist. Sits at the back of the Maple Tree Pub nursing a cold cup of stars. Knows where things are lost.
npcs.gameplayRole: Daily quest giver. Rotates 3 daily quests (PvE, PvP, social).

npcs.lore: Ruler of the Crossroads City. Benevolent but politically cautious. Offers main-story quests.
npcs.gameplayRole: Main campaign quest giver for levels 1-20.

npcs.lore: The King's equal. Handles foreign affairs — which includes dealing with the Outlands PK problem.
npcs.gameplayRole: Offers bounty quests to hunt down PK-flagged players.

npcs.displayName: Grandmaster Kael, High Defender of Camelot
npcs.lore: Leader of the Assembly of Knights. Upholds the Four Virtues. Decides who earns a seat at the Round Table.
npcs.gameplayRole: Knight class trainer. Offers Knight-exclusive tournament quests.

npcs.lore: Ancient hero, one of the earliest adventurers. Runs the Adventurers Guild. Friendly, patient, rarely impressed.
npcs.gameplayRole: Adventurer class trainer. Delivers tutorial quest chain.

npcs.lore: Leader of the Druids, chosen by the Old Wood itself. Speaks to trees. Trees speak back.
One-eyed veteran of a hundred Dragonspine hunts. Keeps a wolf-hound at his heel at all times.
npcs.gameplayRole: Hunter class trainer. Sells bows, arrows, traps.

npcs.lore: Abbot of Cathbad Abbey. Never smiles. Never raises his voice. Strikes like a landslide.

npcs.displayName: Captain Hargrim of the Ghan-Toth Guard
npcs.lore: Commands the city watch. Under standing order from the Queen: any PK-flagged killer who sets foot inside the walls does not leave.
npcs.gameplayRole: Level 60 elite. Spawns alongside 4 patrol guards if any PK player enters hub.

npcs.lore: Fletcher and bowyer. Rumored to have once outshot the Huntmaster at a fair.
Alchemist. Sells potions. Smells faintly of rosemary and burning copper.
npcs.gameplayRole: Consumable merchant. Sells healing potions, mana potions, antidotes.

npcs.lore: Leader of the Foothills bandit warband. A contract target for the Queen's bounty quests. Sometimes offers PKs a quest of their own — for a price.
npcs.gameplayRole: Miniboss. Drops rare loot. Elite PKs can parley with him for alternative quests.
